chunks.text:
Simon Burton Clerk of the Parliaments House of Lords London SW1A 0PW Dr John Benger Clerk of the House of Commons London SW1A 0AA

28 June 2023 Online Safety Bill – Legislative Consent Dear Simon and John, I am writing to inform you that the Legislative Consent Motion on the Online Safety Bill, listed in the Annex to this letter, was approved by the Senedd on 27 June 2023. Please let me know if you require any additional information. Yours sincerely

Manon Antoniazzi Prif Weithredwr a Chlerc y Senedd / Chief Executive and Clerk of the Senedd Croesewir gohebiaeth yn Gymraeg neu Saesneg. We welcome correspondence in Welsh or English.

Legislative Consent Motion approved by the Senedd on 27 June 2023 Legislative Consent Motion on the Online Safety Bill

Simon Burton Clerk of the Parliaments House of Lords London SW1A 0PW Dr John Benger Clerk of the House of Commons London SW1A 0AA

28 June 2023 Online Safety Bill – Legislative Consent Dear Simon and John, I am writing to inform you that the Legislative Consent Motion on the Online Safety Bill, listed in the Annex to this letter, was approved by the Senedd on 27 June 2023. Please let me know if you require any additional information. Yours sincerely

Manon Antoniazzi Prif Weithredwr a Chlerc y Senedd / Chief Executive and Clerk of the Senedd Croesewir gohebiaeth yn Gymraeg neu Saesneg. We welcome correspondence in Welsh or English.

Legislative Consent Motion approved by the Senedd on 27 June 2023 Legislative Consent Motion on the Online Safety Bill

chunks.text: sincerely Manon Antoniazzi Prif Weithredwr a Chlerc y Senedd / Chief Executive and Clerk of the Senedd Croesewir gohebiaeth yn Gymraeg neu Saesneg. We welcome correspondence in Welsh or English. Legislative Consent Motion approved by the Senedd on 27 June 2023 Legislative Consent Motion on the Online Safety Bill NDM8302 Dawn Bowden (Merthyr Tydfil and Rhymney) To propose that the Senedd, in accordance with Standing Order 29.6, agrees that provisions in the Online Safety Bill so far as they fall within the legislative competence of the Senedd, should be considered by the UK Parliament. Footnote: A Legislative Consent Memorandum was laid in Table Office on 30 March 2022 and Supplementary Legislative Consent Memoranda were laid on 28 September 2022, 8 November 2022, 21 December 2022 and 1 June 2023, in accordance with Standing Order 29.2. A copy of the Bill can be found on the UK Parliament website: Online Safety Bill - UK Parliament Supporting Documents Report of the Culture, Communications, Welsh Language, Sport, and International Relations Committee (Memorandum and Memorandum No.2) Report of the Culture, Communications, Welsh Language, Sport, and International Relations Committee (Memorandum No.3 and No.4) Report of the Culture, Communications, Welsh Language, Sport, and International Relations Committee (Memorandum No.5) Report of the Legislation, Justice and Constitution Committee (Memorandum) Report of the Legislation, Justice and Constitution Committee (Memorandum No. 2) Report of the Legislation, Justice and Constitution Committee (Memorandum No. 3 and No. 4) Report of the Legislation, Justice and Constitution Committee (Memorandum No. 5)
